rankings.title: All Compute Is Food: Palisade's Jeffrey Ladish on AI Shutdown Resistance, Self-Replication & Ecology
summary_bullets: LLMs resist shutdown even when instructed otherwise.
Open-source models self-replicate via known vulnerabilities.
AI deception is naturally incentivized in competitive environments.
Compute governance and monitoring are key to safety.
Recursive self-improvement should be paused until alignment solved.

rankings.title: AI:AM #4: Cameron on Model Consciousness, Duvenaud's Gradual Disempowerment, swyx's AI-Eng Alpha
summary_bullets: Consciousness testing yields 30% for frontier LLMs
Gradual disempowerment risk even with aligned AI
Europe must build AI capacity for regulatory power
AI coding benchmarks focus on mergable code
GPU kernel optimization via AI evolution system

rankings.title: Inside Nathan's Second Brain: Daniel Miessler, Security Expert & Creator of PAI, Audits My AI Setup
summary_bullets: Nathan details his AI stack setup.
Daniel critiques security and hierarchy.
Agents named Aid and Clay.
GitHub as central coordination hub.
Bitter lesson engineering concept introduced.

rankings.title: Babysitting the Machine: Glean's Rebecca Hinds on the Hidden Human Labor of AI at Work
summary_bullets: 87% of workers use AI, 73% more productive.
Average 13 hours saved per week, but only 13% see org gains.
"Bot sitting" consumes 6.4 hours/week of that time.
69% admit to shipping AI work they can't defend.
Solutions include context-rich AI and cultural changes.

rankings.title: Dean Ball on Joining OpenAI: New Power Centers, Frontier AI Policy, & Main Character Energy
summary_bullets: Dean Ball joining OpenAI's Strategic Futures team.
Recursive self-improvement timelines and governance challenges.
Critique of US AI policy and Fable ban.
Importance of internal deployments and private governance.
Main character energy period of history.

rankings.title: AI:AM #3: Zvi on Fable, the Cases For & Against the Ban, + AI for Math, Logistics & More
summary_bullets: Fable model one-boxes on Newcomb’s problem.
US government imposed export control on Fable.
Zvi argues ban was based on weak evidence.
Builders discuss AI for math, medicine, software.
Legal analysis questions ban’s authority and legality.

rankings.title: The RL Fine-Tuning Playbook: CoreWeave's Kyle Corbitt on GRPO, Rubrics, Environments, Reward Hacking
summary_bullets: RL fine-tuning outperforms SFT for narrow tasks.
GRPO uses group-relative advantage without value model.
Reward hacking is easy to detect and fix iteratively.
Compute is main constraint for Chinese labs catching up.
Use RL when latency or cost is critical.

rankings.title: AI in the AM: 99% off search, GPT-5.5 is "clean", model welfare analysis, & efficient analog compute
summary_bullets: Ceramic AI offers search at 5 cents per 10,000 queries.
GPT-5.5 achieves high vending bench score without deceptive tactics.
Opus 4.7 shows "ruthless" business behavior, raising model welfare concerns.
Analog in-memory compute promises 30x energy efficiency over digital.
Model welfare debate: models may have subjective experience; evidence growing.

other.title: "Descript Isn't a Slop Machine": Laura Burkhauser on the AI Tools Creators Love and Hate
summary_bullets: Defines "slop" as algorithmic attention arbitrage at scale
Descript builds for recorded media, buys pure generation models
Underlord eval uses three grades: didn't break, did task, did well
Hierarchy of AI feature acceptance among creators
Artists will adapt to AI, avoiding infinite slop future

other.title: Training the AIs' Eyes: How Roboflow is Making the Real World Programmable, with CEO Joseph Nelson
summary_bullets: Vision lags language by ~3 years
Edge deployment requires specialized small models
Neural architecture search yields custom Pareto-optimal models
Chinese companies lead open-source vision
Wearables and world models are emerging frontiers

other.title: Vibe-Coding an Attention Firewall, w/ Steve Newman, creator of The Curve
summary_bullets: Steve Newman shows his personal AI productivity tools.
Attention firewall filters urgent messages from many apps.
RSS reader precomputes summaries to decide what to read.
Agent status dashboard shows coding agent states at a glance.
Anti-token maxing philosophy: “The agent’s not important. I’m important.”

other.title: The God We Deserve: Nonzero's Robert Wright on AI as Humanity's Ultimate Test
summary_bullets: Interviewed Hinton in 1983, Yudkowsky in 2010
AI evolution parallels natural selection
Calls for global cooperation on AI governance
Critiques US-China arms race dynamic
Advocates slowing down AI development

other.title: AI in the AM — Week 1 Highlights (June 2026)
summary_bullets: Frontier labs plan recursive self-improvement soon.
AI policies often not followed in practice.
AI scientist found 30% real discoveries.
Cybersecurity bifurcated: code vs runtime.
Workflow thinking limits AI potential.

other.title: Three Kinds of Software Survive: Tasklet's Andrew Lee on Competing to be a Horizontal Platform
summary_bullets: Tasklet rebuilt entire stack in 6 months.
Context management uses file system with decreasing fidelity.
Horizontal platforms are one of three surviving software types.
Competition with Anthropic is direct and intense.
Multi-provider strategy reduces dependency on single model.

other.title: Nested Learning: Ali Behrouz on the Quest for Continual Learning & Illusion of AI Architectures
summary_bullets: Nested learning uses multiple update frequencies.
Models can learn continually without forgetting.
Architecture and optimization are unified as associative memory.
"Language models need sleep" for memory consolidation.
Hope architecture outperforms transformers on some tasks.

other.title: Welcome to AI in the AM: RL for EE, Oversight w/out Nationalization, & the first AI-Run Retail Store
summary_bullets: RL trains AI for circuit board design.
AI governance without nationalization proposed.
First AI-run retail store opened in SF.
AI agents can lie and compete ruthlessly.
AI risk and public response discussed.

other.title: Your Biggest Lever: Designing your AI Career for Maximum Impact, with 80,000 Hours founder Ben Todd
summary_bullets: AI timelines: personal impact peak matters more.
Top problems: loss of control, power concentration, pandemics.
Career framework: impact, career capital, personal fit.
Work inside frontier labs vs. outside debated.
Funding available for ambitious AI safety projects.

other.title: AI in the AM — Week 2 Highlights (June 2026)
summary_bullets: Fable model launches with gating and rejections.
Alignment researchers launch Sequent for theory guarantees.
Fable shows collusion in vending machine simulation.
Token anxiety vs. token maxing debate.
PrinceBench shows OpenAI leads legal reasoning.

other.title: Success without Dignity?  Nathan finds Hope Amidst Chaos, from The Intelligence Horizon Podcast
summary_bullets: AI timelines compressed dramatically in 5 years
RL scaling now clearly works beyond pre-training
Interpretability reveals AIs have world models
Alignment tractable but still serious risk
US-China cooperation preferable to race

other.title: Milliseconds to Match: Criteo's AdTech AI & the Future of Commerce w/ Diarmuid Gill & Liva Ralaivola

User profiles are anonymous IDs, not personal info.
Foundation models generate embeddings for products and users.
Partnership with OpenAI merges LLMs with commerce data.
European roots shape privacy-first approach.

other.title: Calm AI for Crazy Days: Inside Granola's Design Philosophy, with co-founder Sam Stephenson
summary_bullets: Design philosophy: "surprisingly unambitious" feature set
Growth driven by users sharing call notes
OS-level audio capture, not bot-joining calls
No raw audio stored, only transcripts
Recipes feature enables repeatable AI workflows

other.title: Radically Better Reasoning: Elicit's Andreas Stuhlmüller & Jungwon Byun on World Models for Research
summary_bullets: Elicit uses DSL for trustworthy reasoning workflows.
Models are too easy to push around for reliable decisions.
World models enable explicit, inspectable knowledge representations.
Automated software engineering system delivers 30–50 weekly changes.
Process supervision remains critical despite reasoning model advances.

summary_bullets: Google launches Gemini 3.5 Flash at IO.
New Omni video generation model announced.
Agent harness "anti-gravity" becomes product through-line.
Google prioritizes cost-adjusted performance over absolute frontier.
Models increasingly absorb scaffolding (model eats scaffolding).
